message:
feat(fuse): vendored fuser patch + ioctl trace probe + e2e ioctl coverage

Adds an end-to-end pathway for exercising real ext4/btrfs ioctls
through the FrankenFS FUSE userspace handler, plus a tracer that
distinguishes kernel/VFS rejection from actual handler invocation.

Cargo.toml / Cargo.lock:
 - New [patch.crates-io] entry pointing fuser at vendor/fuser. The
   vendored copy forwards unrestricted ioctls so FIEMAP and
   EXT4_IOC_* reach userspace instead of being short-circuited
   by the upstream crate's allowlist. Lock file now records the
   path source rather than the crates.io checksum.

crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs (+152):
 - New `ioctl_trace_path: Option<PathBuf>` mount parameter; when
   set, the mount server opens the path as an append-only
   `IoctlTraceProbe` (BufWriter<File>) and records every ioctl
   callback as `ino=N cmd=0xHEX in_len=N out_size=N`
 - `IoctlTraceProbe` lazily opens its writer and flushes on drop;
   errors on write are surfaced but do not fail the ioctl itself
   (tracing is a non-mandatory diagnostic)
 - Integrates into the existing Filesystem impl so both managed
   and per-core mount paths pick up the probe uniformly

crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs (+3):
 - Threads `ioctl_trace_path: None` through all three CLI mount
   construction sites (mount_with_fuse, mount_with_managed_fuse,
   mount_with_per_core_fuse). The CLI does not expose the trace
   option to end users -- it is enabled by harness tests only.

crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs (+406):
 - New constants for `FS_IOC_FIEMAP_CMD`, `EXT4_IOC_GETFLAGS_CMD`,
   `EXT4_IOC_SETFLAGS_CMD` matching the kernel layout
 - `read_ioctl_trace` + `trace_contains_cmd` helpers that parse
   the probe's output to assert that a specific command reached
   the handler
 - `try_mount_ffs_with_options` variant that plumbs trace path and
   extra mount options through to ffs-fuse
 - Full-path tests for FIEMAP read-back after extent allocation
   and GETFLAGS/SETFLAGS round-trip through the userspace handler,
   each asserting both the ioctl result and that the trace file
   recorded the expected command code

files.patch:
@@ -3745,6 +3745,7 @@ fn mount_with_fuse(
         read_only: !read_write,
         allow_other,
         auto_unmount,
+        ioctl_trace_path: None,
         worker_threads: 0,
     };
 
@@ -3770,6 +3771,7 @@ fn mount_with_managed_fuse(open_fs: OpenFs, params: &ManagedMountParams<'_>) ->
             read_only: !params.read_write,
             allow_other: params.allow_other,
             auto_unmount: params.auto_unmount,
+            ioctl_trace_path: None,
             worker_threads: 0,
         },
         unmount_timeout: std::time::Duration::from_secs(params.unmount_timeout_secs),
@@ -3832,6 +3834,7 @@ fn mount_with_per_core_fuse(open_fs: OpenFs, params: &ManagedMountParams<'_>) ->
             read_only: !params.read_write,
             allow_other: params.allow_other,
             auto_unmount: params.auto_unmount,
+            ioctl_trace_path: None,
             worker_threads: dispatcher.num_cores() as usize,
         },
         unmount_timeout: std::time::Duration::from_secs(params.unmount_timeout_secs),
